CREATE TABLE date_test (year INT, month INT, day INT);
SELECT make_date(year, month, day) AS created_date FROM date_test;
SELECT make_date(year, month, day) AS created_date FROM date_test WHERE year > 1900 AND month BETWEEN 1 AND 12;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT make_date(year, month, day) FROM date_test) AS date_results WHERE created_date > '2000-01-01';
SELECT (SELECT make_date(year, month, day) FROM date_test WHERE year = 2023) AS future_date, (SELECT make_date(year, month, day) FROM date_test WHERE year = 1992) AS past_date;
SELECT d.created_date, r.ref_date FROM (SELECT make_date(year, month, day) AS created_date FROM date_test) AS d JOIN reference_dates AS r ON d.created_date < r.ref_date;
SELECT CASE WHEN make_date(year, month, day) IS NOT NULL THEN 'Valid Date' ELSE 'Invalid Date' END AS date_status FROM date_test;

WITH date_generated AS (SELECT make_date(year, month, day) AS generated_date FROM date_test) SELECT generated_date FROM date_generated WHERE generated_date > '2000-01-01' ORDER BY generated_date DESC;
